assumptions: The issue concerns consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The intragroup dividend has resulted in a recognised intragroup receivable/payable before or within consolidation accounting.
The risk being considered is foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to a particular risk that could affect profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge accounting for a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Ordinary foreign currency accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 and IFRS 9 provide the non-hedge-accounting model under which exchange differences on foreign currency monetary items, including certain intragroup monetary items in consolidation, are recognised in profit or loss or OCI as applicable.
